summary: B5 tested the occupancy hypothesis from B2: does batching (c>1, rows=c*top_k) lift the fused MoE FFN megakernel out of the single-token occupancy wall? YES. The fused kernel beats the unfused raw Q4_K chain at every batch size, peaking at c=8 (4.47x), with KL~0 and launches/layer 3->1. BUT there is no GGUF concurrent c>1 decode E2E path to deploy it in (qwen35_gguf_bench is c=1-only; GGUF rows>1 = prefill, where WMMA beats the fused GEMV design). The batched-small-rows regime where the fused GEMV megakernel wins is precisely the speculative VERIFY shape (B+1 rows/request, GEMV-appropriate, dispatch-bound). Conclusion: the megakernel's deployment target is the verify/C_B regime (PARO), not GGUF decode. This completes the GGUF megakernel architecture-validation arc (B0-B5).

workload.shape: decode-shaped selected-expert MoE FFN, hidden=2048, ffn_len=512, top_k=8, experts=256
workload.sweep: c (concurrent decode tokens) in {1,2,4,8,16,32}; rows=c*top_k; fused blocks=rows

measurements.note: hot-cache A/B (fair for both paths), per-call ms; fused = 1 launch/layer, unfused raw chain = 3 (dual_gemv + silu + down)
measurements.interpretation: Speedup rises 1.55x -> 4.47x as c grows 1 -> 8 (more blocks = better CU utilization, confirming the occupancy hypothesis), then falls past c=16 as per-block heavy work exceeds CU residency. The c=8 sweet spot (64 blocks on 48 CUs) is the verify-shaped regime.

correctness.fused_vs_unfused_kl: ~0 across all c (numerically identical FFN; both bf16)
correctness.kernel_gate: PASS (B2-certified: oracle-exact on real weights, 1-ULP vs unfused in situ, KL 2.5e-4 vs cpu_reference)

decision.occupancy_hypothesis: CONFIRMED (batching lifts the megakernel; 4.47x at c=8)
decision.blocker: No GGUF concurrent c>1 decode E2E path exists (c=1-only harness; rows>1 = prefill where WMMA beats fused GEMV). The fused GEMV megakernel's winning regime (batched small rows) is the speculative verify shape, not any GGUF inference path.
decision.next: Pivot to the verify/C_B regime (PARO): wire the fused selected-expert FFN megakernel into the speculative verify loop (B+1 rows x concurrent requests = naturally batched small-rows, GEMV-appropriate, dispatch-bound), where launch-reduction (3->1) directly attacks the 931-launch/pass verify wall (MEGAKERNEL.md C_B target). This is the deployable target the whole B0-B5 GGUF arc was de-risking the architecture for.

notes: GGUF megakernel arc B0-B5 complete: B0 golden oracle, B1 fused kernel (correct, row-invariant, 1 launch/layer), B2 wired+certified (correct, not promoted: T16 deployed path + single-token occupancy), B5 batched occupancy CONFIRMED but no GGUF batched-decode deployment surface.
The architecture is validated and correct; the deployment surface is the verify regime.
